rag_rules: Preserve the existing AADL architecture and attach inserted content only within a valid owner scope for that owner kind.
Keep type-scope content separate from implementation-scope content; do not move statements across owners during fusion.
Do not introduce references that are not visible from the chosen owner scope, including implementation-local paths in broader scopes.
generic_rules: Merge by minimally adding the new annex block without altering unrelated features, connections, properties, or declarations.
Retain existing component and implementation structure, ordering, and non-target text unless a scope-safe merge requires a local adjustment.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Implementation-level content belongs in an implementation scope after relevant structural declarations.
Type-level contracts may use only identifiers visible at the component type level, not subcomponent ports or internal implementation names.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-specific logic in a component type owner.
Do not rewrite architecture elements or property associations when only annex fusion is needed.
Do not treat AADL property names or units as in-scope expression identifiers during fusion.
irrelevant_topics: Type-mismatch repair guidance unless diagnostics appear
